2605.10292.title: LeapTS: Rethinking Time Series Forecasting as Adaptive Multi-Horizon Scheduling
2605.10292.paper_summary: Time series forecasting serves as an essential tool for many real-world applications, supporting tasks such as resource optimization and decision-making. Despite significant architectural advancements, most modern models still treat forecasting task as a fixed mapping from history to target horizons. This induces temporal decoupling across future time points and limits the model's ability to adapt to the evolving context as forecasting progresses. In this work, we present LeapTS, a novel framework that reformulates time series forecasting as a dynamic scheduling process over the prediction horizon. Specifically, LeapTS organizes the forecasting process into multi-level decisions using: (1) the hierarchical controller to dynamically select the optimal prediction scale and advancement length at each step, and (2) continuous-time state evolution driven by neural controlled differential equations. Within this process, the controlled update mechanism explicitly couples the irregular temporal dynamics with discrete scheduling feedback. Extensive evaluations on both real-world and synthetic datasets demonstrate that LeapTS improves overall forecasting performance by at least 7.4% while achieving a 2.6$\times$ to 5.3$\times$ inference speedup over representative Transformer-based models. Furthermore, by explicitly tracing the scheduling trajectories, we reveal how the model autonomously adapts its forecasting behavior to capture non-stationary dynamics.
2605.10292.paper_summary_zh: 時間序列預測作為許多現實世界應用的重要工具，支持資源優化和決策等任務。儘管架構上有顯著的進步，但大多數現代模型仍然將預測任務視為從歷史到目標時間範圍的固定映射。這導致未來時間點之間的時間解耦，並限制了模型在預測過程中適應不斷變化的上下文的能力。在這項工作中，我們提出了LeapTS，一個將時間序列預測重新定義為預測範圍內的動態調度過程的新框架。具體而言，LeapTS將預測過程組織為多層決策，使用：(1) 階層控制器在每一步動態選擇最佳預測尺度和推進長度，以及 (2) 由神經控制微分方程驅動的連續時間狀態演變。在這一過程中，受控更新機制明確地將不規則的時間動態與離散調度反饋耦合在一起。在真實世界和合成數據集上的廣泛評估顯示，LeapTS將整體預測性能提高了至少7.4%，同時在代表性的基於Transformer的模型上實現了2.6$\times$到5.3$\times$的推理加速。此外，通過明確追蹤調度軌跡，我們揭示了模型如何自主調整其預測行為以捕捉非平穩動態。
2605.10292.author: Sheng Pan et.al.
2605.10292.authors: Sheng Pan, Ming Jin, Bo Du, Shirui Pan
